food_pairing.name: Brunello di Montalcino with bistecca alla fiorentina
food_pairing.pairing: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG with charcoal-grilled bistecca alla fiorentina
food_pairing.description: Florence's defining wine-and-meat marriage: a thick Chianina T-bone seared over chestnut embers, paired with Brunello's iron-cherry spine and resolved tannins.

food_pairing.name: Chianti Classico with ribollita
food_pairing.pairing: Chianti Classico DOCG with ribollita, the Florentine bean-and-bread stew
food_pairing.description: Florentine cucina povera meets the regional everyday wine. Chianti Classico's bright Sangiovese acidity refreshes the bean stew's heaviness while its herbal lift ech

food_pairing.name: Super Tuscan with trippa alla fiorentina
food_pairing.pairing: Sangiovese-led Super Tuscan with trippa alla fiorentina, Florentine tomato-braised tripe
food_pairing.description: Florence's offal classic finds a serious partner in Sangiovese-led Super Tuscans. The wine's structured tannin cuts through the long-stewed tripe while its herbal ar

food_pairing.name: Vin Santo with cantucci
food_pairing.pairing: Vin Santo with cantucci almond biscotti
food_pairing.description: The canonical Tuscan after-dinner ritual: dunk hard almond biscotti into a small glass of Vin Santo. The wine's apricot-and-walnut depth and oxidative rancio meet th

food_pairing.name: Chianti Classico with crostini di fegatini
food_pairing.pairing: Chianti Classico with crostini di fegatini, Florentine chicken-liver crostini
food_pairing.description: The aperitivo Tuscan rite. Sour-cherry Sangiovese acidity cuts through chicken-liver fat while the wine's tobacco-and-herb register echoes the capers, anchovies and

food_pairing.name: Vernaccia di San Gimignano with lampredotto
food_pairing.pairing: Vernaccia di San Gimignano with lampredotto, Florence's tripe street-food sandwich
food_pairing.description: Florence's working-class street-food meets the city's regional white. Vernaccia's almond-and-salt structure refreshes the brothy fourth-stomach sandwich while its hi

food_pairing.name: Chianti Rufina with pici al ragu
food_pairing.pairing: Chianti Rufina DOCG with hand-rolled pici and meat ragu
food_pairing.description: Cool-climate Chianti Rufina pairs precisely with hand-rolled pici and slow ragu. The high-altitude Sangiovese's sour-cherry lift refreshes the rich meat sauce while

food_pairing.name: Bolgheri Super Tuscan with dry-aged rib-eye
food_pairing.pairing: Bolgheri Cabernet-led blend with dry-aged Chianina rib-eye
food_pairing.description: Florence's modern steakhouses plate dry-aged Chianina that demands Bolgheri's polished Cabernet weight. Graphite-cedar meets dry-aged beef umami.

food_pairing.name: Morellino di Scansano with wild boar pappardelle
food_pairing.pairing: Morellino di Scansano DOCG with pappardelle al cinghiale (wild boar ragu)
food_pairing.description: Maremma's coastal Sangiovese partners the Tuscan boar that haunts the hillsides above its vineyards. Trattoria Mario plates the canonical Florentine pappardelle al c

food_pairing.name: Late-harvest sweet wine with schiacciata fiorentina
food_pairing.pairing: Vin Santo or Pomino Benefizio with schiacciata fiorentina (orange-scented Carnival sweet bread)
food_pairing.description: Florence's Carnival sweet bread, perfumed with orange and dusted with powdered sugar, leans on Vin Santo. Apricot-honey depth doubles the orange peel.
